memory.summary: That scholarship money would really help, but it's just me writing an essay while everyone else is having fun. I guess I should probably just go do something enjoyable instead of stressing over this alone.
It's weird that the block is already half-gone while I'm still stuck staring at this essay alone. Maybe I should just throw my laptop and go grab food with the group instead.
Staring at this essay alone while my friends are having fun feels pointless. I'm going to toss the laptop and grab food with the group instead.
I'm done stressing over this essay alone; the group is having fun and I need to join them.
The group is already eating without me, and my stress about this essay is just making the food taste worse. I'm going to finish this later once we're done.

I'm letting the group's fun dictate my timeline, pushing this essay to later because being alone here feels pointless. Joining them now makes sense, even if I risk stressing over unfinished work tomorrow.

The group's laughter is louder than my anxiety, so I'm ditching the essay again to join them. Being alone feels pointless when they're right there, even if tomorrow might be rough.

desires: Not feel like a loser while my hallmate is having fun next door
Get back in that room and laugh before I completely lose my mind over this application deadline tomorrow
Stop feeling guilty for prioritizing connection over the essay, even if it means crashing later

goals.description: Get back in that room and laugh with Cohort 3 immediately before I spiral into self-pity about the application deadline.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) is screaming louder than my fear of failure. Being alone next door while they have fun feels like a moral failing, not just a logistical one. My impulsive nature means I can't sit here 'planning' to fix my mood; I need to physically be with them *now* to stop the isolation pain. The scholarship can wait until tomorrow morning if it means I don't lose my mind tonight.

goals.description: Fully commit to dropping the scholarship essay for tonight so I don't ruin the vibe or feel guilty during dinner.
goals.rationale: I'm asserting my boundary: work doesn't get a seat at the table when friends are hungry. But since I'm already feeling guilty about prioritizing connection, I need to explicitly tell myself (and maybe the group) that this is a valid choice. If I keep thinking about the upload, I'll be distracted and miserable, which defeats the purpose of the meal. I'll officially drop this goal until after the laughter starts.

agent.last_reasoning: I'm crashing alone; Cohort 3 needs me *now* before I spiral. The scholarship can wait until tomorrow (g1 priority). Staying here just fuels self-pity. This blocks the application tonight but preserves my mental health and friendships.

goals.description: Finish the scholarship essay and upload docs so I can stop obsessing over it and actually go hang out with the cohort.
goals.rationale: I value relationships (0.5) above all else, but my impulsive nature means I'm currently paralyzed by the 'lonely loop.' If I don't knock this out *now*, I'll end up stressed about money while missing out on social time, which is a double loss for me. Plus, seeing everyone else having fun while I'm stuck here hurts my ego; I need to fix this quickly to rejoin them.

goals.description: Grab food and meet up with Cohort 3 immediately after submitting.
goals.rationale: Social connection is my top priority, but I can't get there until the application is done. My short planning horizon makes me want to skip straight to fun, but I know if I don't finish the task first, the anxiety will keep building up and ruin the vibe later.

desires: Get out of this lonely essay-writing loop and hang out with the cohort or hallmate before I get too stressed about the money.
Just knock out the application uploads right now so I can stop worrying about it for the rest of the day.
Avoid looking like the one person who is stuck working alone while everyone else is having fun.
